scams.title: Kabukicho / Roppongi tout 'free drink'
scams.where: Shinjuku Kabukicho, Roppongi, parts of Shibuya
scams.what: Friendly tout offers 'cheap all-you-can-drink' or 'first drink free'; bar bill = 50,000-300,000 JPY ($300-2,000); intimidation if refused; sometimes drink-spiked
scams.avoid: NEVER follow anyone soliciting on the street to a bar. Use only places you researched on Google Maps or Tabelog with reviews. Cash for nightlife (limit damage).

scams.title: Drink spiking (Roppongi)
scams.what: GHB or rohypnol; victim wakes up missing money/cards/phone or assault
scams.avoid: Avoid Roppongi gaijin bars solo. Order bottled drinks. Watch bartender pour. Never accept drinks from strangers.

scams.title: Marc/Alex 'lost wallet' scam
scams.where: Tokyo/Osaka trains, especially targeting solo women
scams.what: Foreign man (often called 'Marc' or 'Alex') in black clothes approaches solo travelers showing phone message saying 'help, lost wallet, need to get to airport'
scams.avoid: Recognize the pattern (black hat, mask, persistent). Ignore him. Real travelers don't beg from strangers in Japan — embassies help.

scams.title: Fake 'monk' donation
scams.where: Near temples — Asakusa Sensoji, Ueno, sometimes Kyoto
scams.what: Person dressed as Buddhist monk hands you bracelet/card and demands donation
scams.avoid: Real monks don't beg on the street. Ignore + walk past. Don't accept anything offered.

scams.title: Model-scout scam
scams.where: Shibuya, Harajuku, Shinjuku — targets young women
scams.what: 'Talent agent' approaches solo woman, offers contract, takes to office where pressured to sign/pay 'training fees' or worse
scams.avoid: Real Japanese model agencies don't street-scout foreigners. Politely decline + walk away. Never go to a private office.

scams.title: 'Charity' donation (laminated photos)
scams.what: Person with binder/laminated photos showing other 'donors' pressures you for large donation
scams.avoid: Laminated props = scam. Real charities never solicit cash from tourists on streets.

scams.title: Counterfeit fashion (Harajuku/Shinjuku)
scams.what: 'Authentic' designer items at low prices = fakes; or fake-marketing pseudo-luxury
scams.avoid: Department stores (Isetan, Mitsukoshi, Takashimaya) only for designer. Vintage/2nd-hand: Komehyo or RagTag are reputable.

scams.title: ATM withdrawal denial / DCC
scams.what: DCC offer ('pay in your home currency') = 5-8% worse rate
scams.avoid: ALWAYS pay in JPY, not your home currency. Use 7-Eleven (Seven Bank) or Japan Post Bank ATMs (lowest fees).

scams.title: Taxi long-route
scams.where: Tokyo airports, less common in metered Tokyo taxis
scams.what: Driver takes 30-min detour through unfamiliar area, you can't tell
scams.avoid: Use Google Maps live tracking. Or use Go Taxi/JapanTaxi app for transparent metered fares.

scams.title: Tax-free purchase confusion
scams.what: Foreigners qualify for tax-free shopping (>5,000 JPY purchase, passport required); some small stores claim no tax-free even when they should offer it
scams.avoid: Bring passport. Look for 'Tax Free' sticker. At Bic Camera/Don Quijote/large stores: ask 'tax-free counter'.
